title: Add Partial Quotients
description: Combine the quotients from decomposed parts of a dividend to find the total quotient.
condition: After dividing decomposed parts of a dividend by the same divisor (e.g., a ÷ d and b ÷ d).
response: Add the partial quotients to get the total quotient.
label: Principle
notes: Relies on distributive property of division over addition.

title: Additive Comparison (More Than)
description: Interpret 'k more than' as adding k to a given quantity.
condition: A word problem states that one quantity is k more than another quantity.
response: Add k to the referenced quantity.
label: Rule
example: If shirts are 5 more than 168 pants, compute $168 + 5 = 173$.
notes: Language-based convention linking 'more than' to addition.

title: Classify Composite Number
description: Determine that a whole number greater than 1 is composite if it has more than two factors.
condition: A whole number greater than 1 with more than two positive factors.
response: State that the number is composite.
label: Concept
example: The number $6$ has factors $1, 2, 3, 6$, so it is **composite**.

title: Classify Prime Number
description: Determine that a whole number greater than 1 is prime if it has exactly two factors: 1 and itself.
condition: A whole number greater than 1 with exactly two positive factors (1 and itself).
response: State that the number is prime.
label: Concept
example: The number $13$ has factors $1$ and $13$, so it is **prime**.

title: Combine Feet and Inches
description: Convert feet to inches and add remaining inches to find total inches.
condition: Given a measurement in feet and inches.
response: Convert feet to inches and add the extra inches.
label: Principle

title: Convert Feet to Inches
description: Convert a measurement in feet to inches using 12 inches per foot.
condition: Given a number of feet.
response: Multiply the number of feet by 12.
label: Rule
example: $4$ ft $= 4 \times 12 = 48$ inches.
notes: Relies on memorized conversion 1 ft = 12 in.

title: Convert Yards to Feet
description: Convert a measurement in yards to feet using 3 feet per yard.
condition: Given a number of yards.
response: Multiply the number of yards by 3.
label: Rule
example: $7$ yd $= 7 \times 3 = 21$ ft.
notes: Relies on memorized conversion 1 yd = 3 ft.

title: Decompose by Place Value
description: Break a number into sums of tens and ones (or other place values).
condition: A multi-digit number in base ten.
response: Express the number as a sum of place-value parts (e.g., tens and ones).
label: Principle

title: Decompose Dividend by Place Value
description: Break a number into place-value parts that are convenient for division.
condition: Given a multi-digit dividend to divide by a single-digit divisor (e.g., 92 ÷ 4).
response: Rewrite the dividend as a sum of place-value parts (e.g., 92 = 80 + 12).
label: Principle
notes: Supports partial quotients strategy.

title: Definition of Composite Number
description: Identify a composite number based on having more than two factors.
condition: A whole number with more than two positive factors.
response: Classify the number as composite.
label: Concept
example: 21 has factors $1,3,7,21$ (more than two), so it is composite.
notes: Paired conceptually with prime definition.

title: Definition of Prime Number
description: Identify a prime number based on having exactly two factors: 1 and itself.
condition: A whole number with exactly two positive factors.
response: Classify the number as prime.
label: Concept
example: 13 has factors $1$ and $13$ only, so it is prime.
notes: Used to contrast with composite numbers.

title: Determine Factor Using Division
description: Check whether one whole number is a factor of another by testing for a remainder of zero.
condition: Given whole numbers $a$ and $b$, and asked whether $a$ is a factor of $b$.
response: Divide $b \div a$ and conclude that $a$ is a factor if the remainder is 0.
label: Principle / Model
example: To test if $7$ is a factor of $28$, compute $28 \div 7 = 4$ with remainder $0$, so $7$ is a factor.

title: Determine Multiple Using Division
description: Check whether a number is a multiple of another by dividing and verifying a whole-number result.
condition: Given whole numbers $a$ and $b$, and asked whether $b$ is a multiple of $a$.
response: Divide $b \div a$ and conclude that $b$ is a multiple if the quotient is a whole number.
label: Principle / Model
example: To test if $27$ is a multiple of $9$, compute $27 \div 9 = 3$, a whole number, so $27$ is a multiple of $9$.

title: Determine Parity in Pattern
description: Determine whether a specific term in an additive pattern is even or odd using pattern structure.
condition: An additive pattern with constant difference and a specified term index.
response: Determine whether the term is even or odd based on the pattern's structure.
label: Principle
example: Pattern: start 3, add 3. Terms alternate odd/even. The 80th term is even.
notes: Parity reasoning may vary ???

title: Divide Multi-Digit by One-Digit
description: Divide a multi-digit number by a one-digit divisor.
condition: Given a division expression with a multi-digit dividend and one-digit divisor (e.g., 720 ÷ 8).
response: Compute the quotient.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Often performed using standard algorithm or place value reasoning.

title: Divide Using Decomposition
description: Divide a number by decomposing it into parts that are divisible by the divisor.
condition: A division expression where the dividend can be decomposed into place-value parts divisible by the divisor.
response: Divide each part and add the partial quotients.
label: Principle

title: Evaluate and Compare to Target Number
description: Determine which expressions equal a given target number.
condition: Given a target number and multiple multiplication expressions.
response: Evaluate each expression and select those equal to the target.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Target: 120. Since $6 \times 20 = 120$ and $30 \times 4 = 120$, select those expressions.
notes: Requires evaluating each option and comparing to the target value.

title: Extend Repeating Pattern
description: Continue a repeating pattern by reproducing the identified repeating unit.
condition: A sequence with a known repeating unit and one or more blanks to fill.
response: Fill each blank with the next element from the repeating unit in order.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the pattern is (triangle, circle, triangle), the next three shapes are triangle, circle, triangle.

title: Find Missing Factor by Division
description: Divide the total area by one side length to find the missing side of a rectangle.
condition: Given area and one side length of a rectangle; missing other side.
response: Compute missing side = area ÷ known side.
label: Principle
example: If area = 150 sq m and width = 5 m, then length = $150 \div 5 = 30$ m.
notes: Inverse relationship between multiplication and division.

title: Generate Terms in Additive Pattern
description: Generate subsequent terms in a number pattern defined by repeatedly adding a constant.
condition: A number pattern defined by a rule of the form 'Add k' starting from an initial term.
response: Add k to the previous term to produce the next term.
label: Principle
example: Start at 3, add 3 each time: $3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Identify Multiples of 4
description: Determine whether a number is a multiple of 4.
condition: A whole number.
response: Determine if it is divisible by 4 (no remainder).
label: Principle
example: 8, 20, and 80 are multiples of 4 because each equals $4 \times n$.

title: Identify Multiples of a Number
description: Determine whether a given number is a multiple of a specified number.
condition: A number n and a specified divisor (e.g., 6).
response: State whether n is a multiple of the divisor.
label: Principle
example: $72$ is a multiple of $6$ because $72 \div 6 = 12$ with no remainder.
notes: May be verified using division or known multiplication facts.

title: Identify Repeating Pattern Unit
description: Recognize the smallest sequence of elements that repeats in a pattern.
condition: A sequence of shapes or objects that repeats in a regular cycle.
response: State the smallest repeating unit of the pattern.
label: Production / Skill
example: In the sequence triangle, circle, triangle, triangle, circle, triangle, the repeating unit is (triangle, circle, triangle).

title: List All Factors of a Number
description: Identify all positive factors of a given whole number.
condition: A given whole number n.
response: List all positive integers that divide n evenly.
label: Principle
example: Factors of 27 are $1, 3, 9, 27$.

title: Match Division Expression to Quotient
description: Evaluate each division expression and select the matching quotient from given choices.
condition: Given multiple division expressions and a list of possible quotients.
response: Compute each quotient and pair it with the correct value from the list.
label: Principle
example: $63 \div 3 = 21$, so match 63 ÷ 3 with 21.
notes: Requires evaluating and comparing results.

title: Multiplicative Comparison (Times as Many)
description: Interpret 'n times as many' as multiplication by n.
condition: A word problem states that one quantity is n times as many as another quantity.
response: Multiply the referenced quantity by n.
label: Rule
example: If pants are 4 times 42 coats, compute $4 \times 42 = 168$.
notes: Language-to-operation mapping convention.

title: Multiply by 10
description: Multiply a whole number by 10 using place value understanding.
condition: Given a whole number multiplied by 10.
response: Append a zero (shift digits left one place) to find the product.
label: Principle
notes: Based on base-10 place value structure.

title: Multiply Two-Digit by One-Digit Number
description: Compute the product of a two-digit number and a one-digit number.
condition: Given an expression of the form (two-digit number) × (one-digit number).
response: Calculate and state the product.
label: Principle
notes: May be executed via distributive property or standard algorithm.

title: Perimeter of a Rectangle
description: Find the perimeter of a rectangle by adding all sides or using the formula 2(l + w).
condition: Given the length and width of a rectangle.
response: Compute perimeter as P = 2(l + w).
label: Principle
example: If l = 6 and w = 4, then $P = 2(6 + 4) = 20$.

title: Regroup Ten as Ones
description: Convert one ten into ten ones to continue division evenly.
condition: A division situation with a remaining ten that cannot be evenly divided.
response: Regroup 1 ten as 10 ones and continue dividing.
label: Principle
example: In $94 \div 2$, regroup 1 ten as 10 ones to make 14 ones; $14 \div 2 = 7$.

title: Select Correct Decomposition Expression
description: Identify an equivalent expression that decomposes the dividend (not the divisor) when dividing.
condition: Asked which expression can be used to compute a division such as 96 ÷ 8.
response: Choose the expression that decomposes the dividend into addends each divided by the same divisor.
label: Concept
notes: Distinguishes valid use of distributive property from incorrect decompositions.

title: Skip Count by 3 on Number Line
description: Label equally spaced points on a number line by counting in increments of 3.
condition: Given a number line with equal intervals representing 3 units each.
response: Label ticks as 0, 3, 6, 9, 12, ...
label: Production / Skill
example: Starting at 0, the next ticks are $3, 6, 9, 12$.
notes: Implicit pattern recognition of equal intervals.

title: Use Linear Units for Perimeter
description: Label perimeter measurements with linear units.
condition: A perimeter measurement of a figure.
response: Express the unit in linear units (e.g., inches).
label: Concept
example: Perimeter = 40 inches.

title: Use Square Units for Area
description: Label area measurements with square units.
condition: An area measurement of a two-dimensional figure.
response: Express the unit as square units (e.g., square inches).
label: Concept
example: Area = 75 square inches.
notes: Unit depends on context but must be squared form.
